publisherName: City of Chicago Department of Water Management
claimSummary: Chicago says homes built before 1986 are especially likely to have lead service lines and directs residents through Lead Safe Chicago water guidance to the inventory and free testing tools.
reviewerNote: Lead-Safe Chicago water guidance page reviewed manually.

publisherName: City of Chicago Department of Water Management
claimSummary: Chicago directs customers to contact lead.safe@cityofchicago.org or 311 for lead service line replacement help and explains that city-assisted program paths are routed through that intake rather than treated as an automatic citywide replacement promise.
reviewerNote: Lead-Safe Chicago replacement guidance reviewed manually for customer guidance.

publisherName: City of Chicago Department of Water Management
claimSummary: Chicago says the city is offering multiple lead service line replacement programs and coordinates replacement work for qualifying properties through separate city-assisted paths rather than one blanket workflow.
reviewerNote: Lead-Safe Chicago replacement guidance reviewed manually for program details.

claimSummary: Fort Wayne says homes built before 1937 are more likely to have lead service lines and directs customers to the city lead page for inventory guidance and next steps.
reviewerNote: Official Fort Wayne lead page reviewed manually for pre-1937 risk and inventory guidance.

claimSummary: Fort Wayne says the lead page provides flushing and filter guidance, explains that owners are responsible for the private pipe to the meter, and routes water-quality questions to city staff instead of treating exposure-reduction guidance as a replacement promise.
reviewerNote: Official Fort Wayne lead page reviewed manually for owner-responsibility and exposure-reduction guidance.

claimSummary: Fort Wayne says its remove-lead program uses city and federal funding to replace service lines in selected neighborhoods and coordinates work with owners when those projects are scheduled.
reviewerNote: Official Fort Wayne remove-lead page reviewed manually for neighborhood program coverage.

claimSummary: Fort Wayne says targeted grant-funded neighborhood replacements are free and that homeowners outside funded areas may still face private-side replacement costs depending on the program path.
reviewerNote: Official Fort Wayne remove-lead page reviewed manually for free neighborhood work versus outside-area cost exposure.

claimSummary: Racine says the city is running a multi-year invitation-based lead service line replacement effort and explains the annual batch structure in its official FAQ materials.
reviewerNote: Official Racine lead service line FAQ reviewed manually.

claimSummary: Racine's official lead-in-water guidance explains ongoing notice and health guidance for homes with lead galvanized or unknown service line risk even when they are not yet in the current replacement batch.
reviewerNote: Official Racine lead-in-water guidance reviewed manually.

claimSummary: Racine says it is running a multi-year private lead service line replacement program and that invited homes can have lead services replaced at no cost in annual city-managed batches.
reviewerNote: Official Racine FAQ reviewed manually for program language.

claimSummary: Racine says invited homeowners can have lead service lines replaced at no cost through the current city-managed batch and invitation workflow.
reviewerNote: Official Racine FAQ reviewed manually for cost language.

claimSummary: Kalamazoo says it tracks lead and copper service line material through its utility lookup tool and that residents can see the current status of their service line records online before deciding whether they are only in a testing path or already in replacement planning.
reviewerNote: Official Kalamazoo lead and copper program page reviewed manually.

claimSummary: Kalamazoo says residents can request free lead and copper testing and receive utility guidance while the city continues inventory and replacement work, and that testing support does not replace the longer replacement workflow.
reviewerNote: Official Kalamazoo free testing page reviewed manually.

claimSummary: Kalamazoo says it is replacing every lead water service line through its ongoing city utility program and ties the effort to water quality and corrosion control work rather than to free-testing-only support.
reviewerNote: Official Kalamazoo lead and copper program page reviewed manually.

claimSummary: Kalamazoo says replacement work is funded through utility rate payers, state revolving fund support, and foundation funding so customers are not charged directly for qualifying city-scheduled replacement work.
reviewerNote: Official Kalamazoo lead and copper program page reviewed manually for cost language.
